title: buildkit, multistage: skip computing stages which dont contribute to target by checking against `dependencyTree`
body:
While performing mult-stage builds buildah does not needs to compute
and run each and every stage it can skip stages which are not used by
`target` stage.

Following commit adds `dependencyTree/dependencyMap` to executor which
calculates and populates the dependencyTree for all the stages and marks
if a particular stage is used by `Target/Final` stage directly or
indirectly and unmarked stages can be skipped.

```Dockerfile
## buildah should skip this stage
FROM alpine
RUN echo "unwanted stage"

FROM alpine as one
RUN echo "wanted stage"

## buildah should skip this stage
FROM alpune
RUN echo "another unwanted stage"

FROM one
RUN echo "wanted stage"
```

Closes: https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/2469

Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [flouthoc] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

This should also improve build perfomance/speed by skipping stage which are not needed. 

PS: I am expecting tests failures cause some tests might be relying on unwanted stages, will retrofit them.

> This should also improve build perfomance/speed by skipping stage which are not needed.
> 
> PS: I am expecting tests failures cause some tests might be relying on unwanted stages, will retrofit them.

As expected I need to retrofit some tests. :\
@nalind Could you PTanotherL. Resolved all the nits.

Some nits about comments, questions in a couple of tests, and this should probably have some tests that ensure that the right things happen when we give additional build contexts the same names as Dockerfile stages.

> Some nits about comments, questions in a couple of tests, and this should probably have some tests that ensure that the right things happen when we give additional build contexts the same names as Dockerfile stages.

Added a new test `build-test skipping unwanted stages with FROM and conflict with additional build context` which should cover this case.
Will rebase this once get we could get LGTM ack. @nalind Could you PTAL again.
Other than the typo being added to a comment in bud.bats, LGTM.

> Other than the typo being added to a comment in bud.bats, LGTM.

Fixed. @rhatdan @TomSweeneyRedHat PTAL

I'll vendor this into podman to verify if we need to retrofit any podman tests as well which might be relying on stages which will be skipped now if that's the case this will cause hiccups in vendor. I'll amend podman tests if i notice any such case.

cc @edsantiago just for info in-case someone vendor buildah before me.
